name: telecommunications_cross_border_data_harbor_ind_col
premise: A Telecommunications response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving telecommunications cross border data harbor ind col. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as individual vs collective under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Luc Sato
agents.identity: I am Luc Sato, accountable for a defensible call in Telecommunications when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays collective for individual.

agents.name: Harper Haddad
agents.identity: I am Harper Haddad, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Telecommunications.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so collective isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of individual.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect collective.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to collective to secure individual under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Luc Sato must decide under individual vs collective. What does Luc Sato do?
